title: Xeriscape Roasted Peruvian Duck Relleno
description: A Pillowy and Cozy Peruvian Dish Featuring Duck Confit Leg, Bhuna, and a Keto-Friendly Twist, all Wrapped in a Delicious Rocoto Relleno.

ingredients.name: Duck Confit Leg (2 legs, 1/2 pound each)
Cedar Plank (1 plank, 12 inches long, 6 inches wide)
Rocoto Peppers (2 peppers, 4 inches long, 2 inches wide)

instructions: Score the duck confit leg in a crisscross pattern, being careful not to cut too deeply into the meat.
Season the duck confit leg with salt and pepper to taste.
Place the duck confit leg on the cedar plank, skin side down.
Roast the duck confit leg in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the skin is crispy and golden brown.
While the duck is roasting, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chopped garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the bhuna and cook, stirring constantly, until heated through.
Remove the duck confit leg from the oven and top with the mozzarella cheese, parmesan cheese, and chopped cilantro.
Return the duck confit leg to the oven and roast for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the duck confit leg from the oven and let cool slightly.
Meanwhile, heat the lime juice in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add the chopped scallions and parsley to the saucepan and cook until the scallions are slightly caramelized, about 2-3 minutes.
To assemble the dish, place the rocoto pepper on a flat surface and scoop out the seeds and membranes.
Fill the rocoto pepper with the roasted duck confit leg, bhuna, and chopped cilantro.
Drizzle the cooked scallions and parsley mixture over the top of the duck confit leg and serve immediately.
